NKJV.1: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the Son of David, the Son of Abraham:
NKJV.2: Abraham begot Isaac, Isaac begot Jacob, and Jacob begot Judah and his brothers.
NKJV.3: Judah begot Perez and Zerah by Tamar, Perez begot Hezron, and Hezron begot Ram.
NKJV.4: Ram begot Amminadab, Amminadab begot Nahshon, and Nahshon begot Salmon.
NKJV.5: Salmon begot Boaz by Rahab, Boaz begot Obed by Ruth, Obed begot Jesse,
NKJV.6: and Jesse begot David the king. David the king begot Solomon by her who had been the wife of Uriah.
NKJV.7: Solomon begot Rehoboam, Rehoboam begot Abijah, and Abijah begot Asa.
NKJV.8: Asa begot Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat begot Joram, and Joram begot Uzziah.
NKJV.9: Uzziah begot Jotham, Jotham begot Ahaz, and Ahaz begot Hezekiah.
NKJV.10: Hezekiah begot Manasseh, Manasseh begot Amon, and Amon begot Josiah.
NKJV.11: Josiah begot Jeconiah and his brothers about the time they were carried away to Babylon.
NKJV.12: And after they were brought to Babylon, Jeconiah begot Shealtiel, and Shealtiel begot Zerubbabel.
NKJV.13: Zerubbabel begot Abiud, Abiud begot Eliakim, and Eliakim begot Azor.
NKJV.14: Azor begot Zadok, Zadok begot Achim, and Achim begot Eliud.
NKJV.15: Eliud begot Eleazar, Eleazar begot Matthan, and Matthan begot Jacob.
NKJV.16: And Jacob begot Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus who is called Christ.
NKJV.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations, from David until the captivity in Babylon are fourteen generations, and from the captivity in Babylon until the Christ are fourteen generations.
NKJV.18: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was as follows: After His mother Mary was betrothed to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child of the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.19: Then Joseph her husband, being a just man, and not wanting to make her a public example, was minded to put her away secretly.
NKJV.20: But while he thought about these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take to you Mary your wife, for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.21: And she will bring forth a Son, and you shall call His name JESUS, for He will save His people from their sins.”
NKJV.22: So all this was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying:
NKJV.23: “Behold, the virgin shall be with child, and bear a Son, and they shall call His name Immanuel,” which is translated, “God with us.”
NKJV.24: Then Joseph, being aroused from sleep, did as the angel of the Lord commanded him and took to him his wife,
NKJV.25: and did not know her till she had brought forth her firstborn Son. And he called His name JESUS.

ESV.1: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
ESV.2: Abraham was the father of Isaac, and Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
ESV.3: and Judah the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, and Perez the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Ram,
ESV.4: and Ram the father of Amminadab, and Amminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon,
ESV.5: and Salmon the father of Boaz by Rahab, and Boaz the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse,
ESV.6: and Jesse the father of David the king. And David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah,
ESV.7: and Solomon the father of Rehoboam, and Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asaph,
ESV.8: and Asaph the father of Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah,
ESV.9: and Uzziah the father of Jotham, and Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
ESV.10: and Hezekiah the father of Manasseh, and Manasseh the father of Amos,  and Amos the father of Josiah,
ESV.11: and Josiah the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
ESV.12: And after the deportation to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel,  and Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,
ESV.13: and Zerubbabel the father of Abiud, and Abiud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor,
ESV.14: and Azor the father of Zadok, and Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud,
ESV.15: and Eliud the father of Eleazar, and Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob,
ESV.16: and Jacob the father of Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom Jesus was born, who is called Christ.
ESV.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations, and from David to the deportation to Babylon fourteen generations, and from the deportation to Babylon to the Christ fourteen generations.
ESV.18: Now the birth of Jesus Christ  took place in this way. When his mother Mary had been betrothed  to Joseph, before they came together she was found to be with child from the Holy Spirit.
ESV.19: And her husband Joseph, being a just man and unwilling to put her to shame, resolved to divorce her quietly.
ESV.20: But as he considered these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, do not fear to take Mary as your wife, for that which is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
ESV.21: She will bear a son, and you shall call his name Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.”
ESV.22: All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had spoken by the prophet:
ESV.23: “Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and they shall call his name Immanuel”  (which means, God with us).
ESV.24: When Joseph woke from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord commanded him: he took his wife,
ESV.25: but knew her not until she had given birth to a son. And he called his name Jesus.

NASB.1: The record of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah, the son of David, the son of Abraham:
NASB.2: Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers.
NASB.3: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, Perez was the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Ram.
NASB.4: Ram was the father of Amminadab, Amminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon.
NASB.5: Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab, Boaz was the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse.
NASB.6: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon by Bathsheba who had been the wife of Uriah.
NASB.7: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asa.
NASB.8: Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah.
NASB.9: Uzziah was the father of Jotham, Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah.
NASB.10: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh, Manasseh the father of Amon, and Amon the father of Josiah.
NASB.11: Josiah became the father of Jeconiah and his brothers, at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
NASB.12: After the deportation to Babylon: Jeconiah became the father of Shealtiel, and Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel.
NASB.13: Zerubbabel was the father of Abihud, Abihud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor.
NASB.14: Azor was the father of Zadok, Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud.
NASB.15: Eliud was the father of Eleazar, Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob.
NASB.16: Jacob was the father of Joseph the husband of Mary, by whom Jesus was born, who is called the Messiah.
NASB.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; from David to the deportation to Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the deportation to Babylon to the Messiah, fourteen generations.
NASB.18: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was as follows: when His mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph, before they came together she was found to be with child by the Holy Spirit.
NASB.19: And Joseph her husband, being a righteous man and not wanting to disgrace her, planned to send her away secretly.
NASB.20: But when he had considered this, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, 'Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife; for the Child who has been conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.
NASB.21: 'She will bear a Son; and you shall call His name Jesus, for He will save His people from their sins.'
NASB.22: Now all this took place to fulfill what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
NASB.23: 'BEHOLD, THE VIRGIN SHALL BE WITH CHILD AND SHALL BEAR A SON, AND THEY SHALL CALL HIS NAME IMMANUEL,' which translated means, 'GOD WITH US.'
NASB.24: And Joseph awoke from his sleep and did as the angel of the Lord commanded him, and took [Mary] as his wife,
NASB.25: but kept her a virgin until she gave birth to a Son; and he called His name Jesus.

NLT.1: This is a record of the ancestors of Jesus the Messiah, a descendant of David and of Abraham :
NLT.2:
Abraham was the father of Isaac.
Isaac was the father of Jacob.
Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
NLT.3:
Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah (whose mother was Tamar).
Perez was the father of Hezron.
Hezron was the father of Ram.
NLT.4:
Ram was the father of Amminadab.
Amminadab was the father of Nahshon.
Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
NLT.5:
Salmon was the father of Boaz (whose mother was Rahab).
Boaz was the father of Obed (whose mother was Ruth).
Obed was the father of Jesse.
NLT.6:
Jesse was the father of King David.
David was the father of Solomon (whose mother was Bathsheba, the widow of Uriah).
NLT.7:
Solomon was the father of Rehoboam.
Rehoboam was the father of Abijah.
Abijah was the father of Asa.
NLT.8:
Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat.
Jehoshaphat was the father of Jehoram.
Jehoram was the father of Uzziah.
NLT.9:
Uzziah was the father of Jotham.
Jotham was the father of Ahaz.
Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
NLT.10:
Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh.
Manasseh was the father of Amon.
Amon was the father of Josiah.
NLT.11: Josiah was the father of Jehoiachin and his brothers (born at the time of the exile to Babylon).
NLT.12:
After the Babylonian exile:
Jehoiachin was the father of Shealtiel.
Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
NLT.13:
Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud.
Abiud was the father of Eliakim.
Eliakim was the father of Azor.
NLT.14:
Azor was the father of Zadok.
Zadok was the father of Akim.
Akim was the father of Eliud.
NLT.15:
Eliud was the father of Eleazar.
Eleazar was the father of Matthan.
Matthan was the father of Jacob.
NLT.16:
Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary.
Mary gave birth to Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
NLT.17: All those listed above include fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen from David to the Babylonian exile, and fourteen from the Babylonian exile to the Messiah.
NLT.18: This is how Jesus the Messiah was born. His mother, Mary, was engaged to be married to Joseph. But before the marriage took place, while she was still a virgin, she became pregnant through the power of the Holy Spirit.
NLT.19: Joseph, to whom she was engaged, was a righteous man and did not want to disgrace her publicly, so he decided to break the engagement quietly.
NLT.20: As he considered this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream. “Joseph, son of David,” the angel said, “do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife. For the child within her was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
NLT.21: And she will have a son, and you are to name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.”
NLT.22: All of this occurred to fulfill the Lord’s message through his prophet:
NLT.23:
“Look! The virgin will conceive a child!
She will give birth to a son,
and they will call him Immanuel,
which means ‘God is with us.’”
NLT.24: When Joseph woke up, he did as the angel of the Lord commanded and took Mary as his wife.
NLT.25: But he did not have sexual relations with her until her son was born. And Joseph named him Jesus.

WEB.1: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ,  the son of David, the son of Abraham.
WEB.2: Abraham became the father of Isaac. Isaac became the father of Jacob. Jacob became the father of Judah and his brothers.
WEB.3: Judah became the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar. Perez became the father of Hezron. Hezron became the father of Ram.
WEB.4: Ram became the father of Amminadab. Amminadab became the father of Nahshon. Nahshon became the father of Salmon.
WEB.5: Salmon became the father of Boaz by Rahab. Boaz became the father of Obed by Ruth. Obed became the father of Jesse.
WEB.6: Jesse became the father of King David. David became the father of Solomon by her who had been Uriah’s wife.
WEB.7: Solomon became the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam became the father of Abijah. Abijah became the father of Asa.
WEB.8: Asa became the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat became the father of Joram. Joram became the father of Uzziah.
WEB.9: Uzziah became the father of Jotham. Jotham became the father of Ahaz. Ahaz became the father of Hezekiah.
WEB.10: Hezekiah became the father of Manasseh. Manasseh became the father of Amon. Amon became the father of Josiah.
WEB.11: Josiah became the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the exile to Babylon.
WEB.12: After the exile to Babylon, Jechoniah became the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel became the father of Zerubbabel.
WEB.13: Zerubbabel became the father of Abiud. Abiud became the father of Eliakim. Eliakim became the father of Azor.
WEB.14: Azor became the father of Zadok. Zadok became the father of Achim. Achim became the father of Eliud.
WEB.15: Eliud became the father of Eleazar. Eleazar became the father of Matthan. Matthan became the father of Jacob.
WEB.16: Jacob became the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, from whom was born Jesus,  who is called Christ.
WEB.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; from David to the exile to Babylon fourteen generations; and from the carrying away to Babylon to the Christ, fourteen generations.
WEB.18: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was like this; for after his mother, Mary, was engaged to Joseph, before they came together, she was found pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
WEB.19: Joseph, her husband, being a righteous man, and not willing to make her a public example, intended to put her away secretly.
WEB.20: But when he thought about these things, behold,  an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, don’t be afraid to take to yourself Mary, your wife, for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.
WEB.21: She shall give birth to a son. You shall call his name Jesus, for it is he who shall save his people from their sins.”
WEB.22: Now all this has happened, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying,
WEB.23: “Behold, the virgin shall be with child, and shall give birth to a son. They shall call his name Immanuel”; which is, being interpreted, “God with us.”
WEB.24: Joseph arose from his sleep, and did as the angel of the Lord commanded him, and took his wife to himself;
WEB.25: and didn’t know her sexually until she had given birth to her firstborn son. He named him Jesus.

CSB17.1: An <sup>ⓒ</sup> account <sup>ⓓ</sup> of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the Son of David, <sup>ⓔ</sup> the Son of Abraham: <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: Abraham fathered [1] Isaac, Isaac fathered Jacob, Jacob fathered Judah and his brothers, ⓖ
CSB17.3: Judah fathered Perez and Zerah by Tamar, Perez fathered Hezron, Hezron fathered Aram, ⓗ
CSB17.4: Aram fathered Amminadab, Amminadab fathered Nahshon, Nahshon fathered Salmon,
CSB17.5: Salmon fathered Boaz by Rahab, Boaz fathered Obed by Ruth, Obed fathered Jesse, ⓘ
CSB17.6: and Jesse fathered King David. David fathered Solomon [2] by Uriah’s wife, ⓙ
CSB17.7: Solomon fathered Rehoboam, Rehoboam fathered Abijah, Abijah fathered Asa, [3]
CSB17.8: Asa [4] fathered Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat fathered Joram, [5] Joram fathered Uzziah, ⓚ
CSB17.9: Uzziah fathered Jotham, Jotham fathered Ahaz, Ahaz fathered Hezekiah,
CSB17.10: Hezekiah fathered Manasseh, Manasseh fathered Amon, [6] Amon fathered Josiah, ⓛ
CSB17.11: and Josiah fathered Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon. ⓜ
CSB17.12: After the exile to Babylon Jeconiah fathered Shealtiel, Shealtiel fathered Zerubbabel,
CSB17.13: Zerubbabel fathered Abiud, Abiud fathered Eliakim, Eliakim fathered Azor, ⓝ
CSB17.14: Azor fathered Zadok, Zadok fathered Achim, Achim fathered Eliud,
CSB17.15: Eliud fathered Eleazar, Eleazar fathered Matthan, Matthan fathered Jacob,
CSB17.16: and Jacob fathered Joseph the husband of Mary, who gave birth to Jesus who is called the Christ. ⓐ
CSB17.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations; and from David until the exile to Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the exile to Babylon until the Christ, fourteen generations. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.18: The birth of Jesus Christ came about this way: After his mother Mary had been engaged <sup>[7]</sup> to Joseph, it was discovered before they came together that she was pregnant from the Holy Spirit. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.19: So her husband Joseph, being a righteous man, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and not wanting to disgrace her publicly, decided to divorce her secretly. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.20: But after he had considered these things, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, don’t be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because what has been conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.21: She will give birth to a son, and you are to name him Jesus, <sup>ⓖ</sup> because he will save his people from their sins.” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.22: Now all this took place to fulfill what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
CSB17.23:
See, the virgin will become pregnant
and give birth to a son,
and they will name him Immanuel, [8]
which is translated “God is with us.” ⓘ
CSB17.24: When Joseph woke up, he did as the Lord’s angel had commanded him. He married her <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.25: but did not have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. <sup>[9]</sup> And he named him Jesus. <sup>ⓚ</sup>

AMP.1: The record of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah, the son (descendant) of David, the son (descendant) of Abraham:
AMP.2: Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers [who became the twelve tribes of Israel].
AMP.3: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, Perez was the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Ram.
AMP.4: Ram was the father of Aminadab, Aminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon.
AMP.5: Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab, Boaz was the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse.
AMP.6:
Jesse was the father of David the king.
David was the father of Solomon by Bathsheba who had been the wife of Uriah.
AMP.7: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asa.
AMP.8: Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah.
AMP.9: Uzziah was the father of Jotham, Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah.
AMP.10: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh, Manasseh the father of Amon, and Amon the father of Josiah.
AMP.11: Josiah became the father of Jeconiah [also called Coniah and Jehoiachin] and his brothers, at the time of the deportation (exile) to Babylon.
AMP.12: After the deportation to Babylon: Jeconiah became the father of Shealtiel, and Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel.
AMP.13: Zerubbabel was the father of Abihud, Abihud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor.
AMP.14: Azor was the father of Zadok, Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud.
AMP.15: Eliud was the father of Eleazar, Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob.
AMP.16: Jacob was the father of Joseph the husband of Mary, by whom Jesus was born, who is called the Messiah (Christ).
AMP.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen; from David to the Babylonian deportation (exile), fourteen generations; and from the Babylonian deportation to the Messiah, fourteen generations.
AMP.18: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was as follows: when His mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph, before they came together she was found to be with child by [the power of] the Holy Spirit.
AMP.19: And Joseph her [promised] husband, being a just and righteous man and not wanting to expose her publicly to shame, planned to send her away and divorce her quietly.
AMP.20: But after he had considered this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, "Joseph, descendant of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for the Child who has been conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.
AMP.21: She will give birth to a Son, and you shall name Him Jesus (The LORD is salvation), for He will save His people from their sins."
AMP.22: All this happened in order to fulfill what the Lord had spoken through the prophet [Isaiah]:
AMP.23: "<e>Behold, the</e><e>virgin shall be with child and give birth to a Son, and they shall call His name Immanuel</e>"—which, when translated, means, "<e>God with us</e>."
AMP.24: Then Joseph awoke from his sleep and did as the angel of the Lord had commanded him, and he took Mary [to his home] as his wife,
AMP.25: but he kept her a virgin until she had given birth to a Son [her firstborn child]; and he named Him Jesus (The LORD is salvation).

MSG.1: The family tree of Jesus Christ, David's son, Abraham's son:
MSG.2: Abraham had Isaac, Isaac had Jacob, Jacob had Judah and his brothers,
MSG.3: Judah had Perez and Zerah (the mother was Tamar), Perez had Hezron, Hezron had Aram,
MSG.4: Aram had Amminadab, Amminadab had Nahshon, Nahshon had Salmon,
MSG.5: Salmon had Boaz (his mother was Rahab), Boaz had Obed (Ruth was the mother), Obed had Jesse,
MSG.6: Jesse had David, and David became king. David had Solomon (Uriah's wife was the mother),
MSG.7: Solomon had Rehoboam, Rehoboam had Abijah, Abijah had Asa,
MSG.8: Asa had Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat had Joram, Joram had Uzziah,
MSG.9: Uzziah had Jotham, Jotham had Ahaz, Ahaz had Hezekiah,
MSG.10: Hezekiah had Manasseh, Manasseh had Amon, Amon had Josiah,
MSG.11: Josiah had Jehoiachin and his brothers, and then the people were taken into the Babylonian exile.
MSG.12: When the Babylonian exile ended, Jehoiachin had Shealtiel, Shealtiel had Zerubbabel,
MSG.13: Zerubbabel had Abiud, Abiud had Eliakim, Eliakim had Azor,
MSG.14: Azor had Zadok, Zadok had Achim, Achim had Eliud,
MSG.15: Eliud had Eleazar, Eleazar had Matthan, Matthan had Jacob,
MSG.16: Jacob had Joseph, Mary's husband, the Mary who gave birth to Jesus, the Jesus who was called Christ.
MSG.17: There were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, another fourteen from David to the Babylonian exile, and yet another fourteen from the Babylonian exile to Christ.
MSG.18: The birth of Jesus took place like this. His mother, Mary, was engaged to be married to Joseph. Before they came to the marriage bed, Joseph discovered she was pregnant. (It was by the Holy Spirit, but he didn't know that.)
MSG.19: Joseph, chagrined but noble, determined to take care of things quietly so Mary would not be disgraced.
MSG.20: While he was trying to figure a way out, he had a dream. God's angel spoke in the dream: "Joseph, son of David, don't hesitate to get married. Mary's pregnancy is Spirit-conceived. God's Holy Spirit has made her pregnant.
MSG.21: She will bring a son to birth, and when she does, you, Joseph, will name him Jesus—'God saves'—because he will save his people from their sins."
MSG.22: This would bring the prophet's embryonic sermon to full term:
MSG.23: Watch for this—a virgin will get pregnant and bear a son; They will name him Emmanuel (Hebrew for "God is with us").
MSG.24: Then Joseph woke up. He did exactly what God's angel commanded in the dream: He married Mary.
MSG.25: But he did not consummate the marriage until she had the baby. He named the baby Jesus.

NIV.1:
The Genealogy of Jesus
A record of the genealogy of Jesus Christ the son of David, the son of Abraham:
NIV.2:
Abraham was the father of Isaac,
Isaac the father of Jacob,
Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
NIV.3:
Judah the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar,
Perez the father of Hezron,
Hezron the father of Ram,
NIV.4:
Ram the father of Amminadab,
Amminadab the father of Nahshon,
Nahshon the father of Salmon,
NIV.5:
Salmon the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab,
Boaz the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth,
Obed the father of Jesse,
NIV.6:
and Jesse the father of King David.
David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been Uriah’s wife,
NIV.7:
Solomon the father of Rehoboam,
Rehoboam the father of Abijah,
Abijah the father of Asa,
NIV.8:
Asa the father of Jehoshaphat,
Jehoshaphat the father of Jehoram,
Jehoram the father of Uzziah,
NIV.9:
Uzziah the father of Jotham,
Jotham the father of Ahaz,
Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
NIV.10:
Hezekiah the father of Manasseh,
Manasseh the father of Amon,
Amon the father of Josiah,
NIV.11: and Josiah the father of Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon.
NIV.12:
After the exile to Babylon:
Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel,
Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,
NIV.13:
Zerubbabel the father of Abiud,
Abiud the father of Eliakim,
Eliakim the father of Azor,
NIV.14:
Azor the father of Zadok,
Zadok the father of Akim,
Akim the father of Eliud,
NIV.15:
Eliud the father of Eleazar,
Eleazar the father of Matthan,
Matthan the father of Jacob,
NIV.16: and Jacob the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.
NIV.17: Thus there were fourteen generations in all from Abraham to David, fourteen from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen from the exile to the Christ.
NIV.18:
The Birth of Jesus Christ
This is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about: His mother Mary was pledged to be married to Joseph, but before they came together, she was found to be with child through the Holy Spirit.
NIV.19: Because Joseph her husband was a righteous man and did not want to expose her to public disgrace, he had in mind to divorce her quietly.
NIV.20: But after he had considered this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary home as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
NIV.21: She will give birth to a son, and you are to give him the name Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
NIV.22: All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had said through the prophet:
NIV.23: “The virgin will be with child and will give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel”—which means, “God with us.”
NIV.24: When Joseph woke up, he did what the angel of the Lord had commanded him and took Mary home as his wife.
NIV.25: But he had no union with her until she gave birth to a son. And he gave him the name Jesus.

NRSVCE.1: An account of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
NRSVCE.2: Abraham was the father of Isaac, and Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
NRSVCE.3: and Judah the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, and Perez the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Aram,
NRSVCE.4: and Aram the father of Aminadab, and Aminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon,
NRSVCE.5: and Salmon the father of Boaz by Rahab, and Boaz the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse,
NRSVCE.6: and Jesse the father of King David. And David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah,
NRSVCE.7: and Solomon the father of Rehoboam, and Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asaph,
NRSVCE.8: and Asaph the father of Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah,
NRSVCE.9: and Uzziah the father of Jotham, and Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
NRSVCE.10: and Hezekiah the father of Manasseh, and Manasseh the father of Amos, and Amos the father of Josiah,
NRSVCE.11: and Josiah the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
NRSVCE.12: And after the deportation to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Salathiel, and Salathiel the father of Zerubbabel,
NRSVCE.13: and Zerubbabel the father of Abiud, and Abiud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor,
NRSVCE.14: and Azor the father of Zadok, and Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud,
NRSVCE.15: and Eliud the father of Eleazar, and Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob,
NRSVCE.16: and Jacob the father of Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Messiah.
NRSVCE.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David to the deportation to Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the deportation to Babylon to the Messiah, fourteen generations.
NRSVCE.18: Now the birth of Jesus the Messiah took place in this way. When his mother Mary had been engaged to Joseph, but before they lived together, she was found to be with child from the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.19: Her husband Joseph, being a righteous man and unwilling to expose her to public disgrace, planned to dismiss her quietly.
NRSVCE.20: But just when he had resolved to do this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for the child conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.21: She will bear a son, and you are to name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.”
NRSVCE.22: All this took place to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
NRSVCE.23:
“Look, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son,
and they shall name him Emmanuel,”
which means, “God is with us.”
NRSVCE.24: When Joseph awoke from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord commanded him; he took her as his wife,
NRSVCE.25: but had no marital relations with her until she had borne a son; and he named him Jesus.

NET.1: This is the record of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
NET.2: Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac the father of Jacob, Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
NET.3: Judah the father of Perez and Zerah (by Tamar), Perez the father of Hezron, Hezron the father of Ram,
NET.4: Ram the father of Amminadab, Amminadab the father of Nahshon, Nahshon the father of Salmon,
NET.5: Salmon the father of Boaz (by Rahab), Boaz the father of Obed (by Ruth), Obed the father of Jesse,
NET.6: and Jesse the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon (by the wife of Uriah ),
NET.7: Solomon the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam the father of Abijah, Abijah the father of Asa,
NET.8: Asa the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, Joram the father of Uzziah,
NET.9: Uzziah the father of Jotham, Jotham the father of Ahaz, Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
NET.10: Hezekiah the father of Manasseh, Manasseh the father of Amon, Amon the father of Josiah,
NET.11: and Josiah the father of Jeconiah and his brothers, at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
NET.12: After the deportation to Babylon, Jeconiah became the father of Shealtiel, Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,
NET.13: Zerubbabel the father of Abiud, Abiud the father of Eliakim, Eliakim the father of Azor,
NET.14: Azor the father of Zadok, Zadok the father of Achim, Achim the father of Eliud,
NET.15: Eliud the father of Eleazar, Eleazar the father of Matthan, Matthan the father of Jacob,
NET.16: and Jacob the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, by whom Jesus was born, who is called Christ.
NET.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations, and from David to the deportation to Babylon, fourteen generations, and from the deportation to Babylon to Christ, fourteen generations.
NET.18: Now the birth of Jesus Christ happened this way. While his mother Mary was engaged to Joseph, but before they came together, she was found to be pregnant through the Holy Spirit.
NET.19: Because Joseph, her husband to be, was a righteous man, and because he did not want to disgrace her, he intended to divorce her privately.
NET.20: When he had contemplated this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
NET.21: She will give birth to a son and you will name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
NET.22: This all happened so that what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet would be fulfilled:
NET.23: “Look! The virgin will conceive and bear a son, and they will call him Emmanuel,” which means “God with us.”
NET.24: When Joseph awoke from sleep he did what the angel of the Lord told him. He took his wife,
NET.25: but did not have marital relations with her until she gave birth to a son, whom he named Jesus.
